column2: The main earner suffers a serious injury at work and cannot return for two months.
column3: "Personal accident cover pays a lump sum when the breadwinner can no longer work \u2014 it keeps the household going."

column2: An unexpected medical visit adds unexpected costs.
column3: Even when healthy you should always plan for unexpected health costs.

column2: An unexpected medical condition requires immediate surgery.
column3: Serious health emergencies can be very expensive.

column2: A family member requires several days of hospital care.
column3: Medical emergencies can lead to unexpected costs.

column2: A family member is admitted to hospital for several days for observation and treatment.
column3: Hospital cash cover pays out daily during a hospital stay

column2: A short illness made you unable to go to work for a few days reducing your monthly pay.
column3: Short shocks can hurt income.

column2: A neighbour or relative passed away. Community tradition requires a contribution.
column3: Social obligations are real financial costs. Plan for them.

column2: Unexpected hospital stay.
column3: Medical savings matter.

column2: Player cannot work.
column3: Income interruption planning.

column2: Ongoing treatment increases healthcare FixedAmounts.
column3: Chronic illness increases expenses.

column2: Family member requires treatment.
column3: Health insurance reduces financial shocks.

column2: You are ill but medicine is currently scarce and expensive as a result of policies.
column3: Crises can increase healthcare costs

column2: A family member has been diagnosed with a serious ailments and will need long term healthcare
column3: Chronic illness increases expenses.

column2: A health crisis limits economic activity.
column3: Macro health events affect income.

column2: A parent's hospital stay means school fees cannot be paid on time this term.
column3: "Education insurance only pays out on the death of a parent \u2014 hospitalisation is not covered. This is why savings matter too."

column2: A serious accident leaves the breadwinner unable to work for months.
column3: Severe injuries can disrupt household income.

column2: A workplace accident leaves the breadwinner unable to work temporarily.
column3: Personal accident insurance can protect income.

column2: A sudden freeze damages vulnerable crops.
column3: Cold snaps are rare but destructive to crops that aren't hardy enough. Planning your planting calendar around seasonal risk reduces your exposure.

column2: Sudden flooding sweeps through the area, damaging property.
column3: Flash floods arrive without warning and damage everything in their path. Home and property insurance is the only financial tool that can absorb losses of this scale.

column2: A violent hailstorm batters fields, destroying crops.
column3: Insurance is even more important during rough weather

column2: Days of heavy rain damage buildings and infrastructure.
column3: Heavy rain damages structures and infrastructure that took years to build. Home insurance covers repair costs that savings alone rarely can.

column2: A lightning strike damages equipment or structures.
column3: "Low-probability events still happen. Insurance isn't about expecting the worst \u2014 it's about being protected when the unexpected occurs."

column2: Prolonged dry conditions reduce water for crops and livestock.
column3: Drought can reduce income for months at a time. Households without savings or insurance are forced into debt just to survive a dry season.

column2: Powerful winds damage buildings and exposed equipment.
column3: Wind damage is unpredictable and often underestimated. Home insurance covers structural repairs that would otherwise drain an entire household budget.

column2: The household breadwinner passes away, severely impacting income.
column3: Insurance can protect families from complete income loss and those left behind can still pursue an education.

column2: School fees increase unexpectedly and must be paid immediately.
column3: Education expenses require planning and savings.

column2: The school sent home a supplementary fee notice. It must be paid or the child stays home.
column3: Education costs are unpredictable. Building a small school buffer helps.

column2: Banks reduce lending, limiting access to credit.
column3: When banks reduce lending, those without savings have nowhere to turn. An emergency fund is your own personal credit line that no bank can take away.

column2: Currency weakness raises the cost of imports.
column3: A weak currency raises the price of imported goods even when your income stays the same. Households feel the squeeze in groceries, fuel, and medication first.

column2: Government policy blocks access to export markets.
column3: Government policy can close markets overnight with no warning. Farmers and traders who depend on a single market are most vulnerable to this kind of disruption.

column2: Rising fuel prices increase transport costs.
column3: "Rising fuel prices ripple through every part of the economy. Transport costs, food prices, and utility bills all rise together \u2014 squeezing household budgets from every direction."

column2: Costs increase suddenly.
column3: Inflation erodes purchasing power.

column2: The ward committee collected an unannounced community levy this month.
column3: Unexpected community demands can disrupt your budget.

column2: Loan costs rise.
column3: Debt risk must be managed.

column2: Kombi and ZUPCO fares have jumped overnight. Getting to work costs more.
column3: Sudden cost changes can strain a tight budget quickly.

column2: A loan repayment is demanded earlier than expected.
column3: Emergency funds are critical.

column2: A sudden oversupply causes crop prices to fall just after your harvest.
column3: Good harvests don't always guarantee good returns. Timing matters.

column2: You were side swipped by a kombi at an intersection.
column3: Motor insurance protects against road accidents. There are various types of motor insurance so pick the right one for you

column2: Your vehicle is stolen overnight from outside your home.
column3: Vehicle theft can cause major financial loss.

column2: The main earner dies unexpectedly in an accident, leaving the household without its primary income.
column3: "Personal Accident Cover pays a lump sum when the breadwinner dies \u2014 it gives the family time to recover financially."

column2: A close relative passes away and the family contributes to funeral costs.
column3: Unexpected family responsibilities can strain finances.

column2: The passing of an elderly family member requires significant funeral expenses.
column3: Planning for end-of-life expenses can protect families.

column2: A cooking accident causes a fire that damages part of the home.
column3: Home insurance protects against property damage.

column2: Heavy rains damage the roof of the house.
column3: Extreme weather can damage property

column2: Pests destroy crops.
column3: Prevention reduces risk.

column2: Poor storage conditions cause mold in stored crops.
column3: Improper storage destroys value that took months to grow. Investing in safe, dry storage facilities is part of the cost of farming.

column2: Water must be purchased to sustain farming operations.
column3: Resource shortages increase expenses

column2: Essential farming equipment suddenly fails.
column3: A broken machine at the wrong time can delay an entire harvest. Keeping an equipment maintenance fund prevents one breakdown from becoming a catastrophe.

column2: Contaminated feed causes illness among animals.
column3: Contaminated feed enters the food chain invisibly. Sourcing from trusted suppliers and keeping records protects both animals and finances.

column2: Fertilizer prices surge, increasing production costs.
column3: Input costs can rise sharply and without warning. Farmers who buy inputs in advance during stable periods protect their margins.

column2: Waterlogged fields delay planting and reduce crop yields.
column3: Extreme weather can damage production

column2: Storm damage requires urgent repairs to local infrastructure.
column3: Disaster recovery can create unexpected expenses

column2: Disease spreads and livestock are lost.
column3: Disease outbreaks can destroy assets

column2: A disease outbreak spreads among livestock.
column3: A single disease outbreak can wipe out months of farming income. Livestock insurance covers losses that no amount of savings can easily absorb.

column2: Drought conditions leave livestock underfed and weak.
column3: Drought affects both crops and animals

column2: Public health restrictions force markets to close.
column3: Public health crises affect trade

column2: An export disruption floods local markets with goods.
column3: Policy changes can distort markets

column2: Disease concerns lead to restrictions on livestock sales.
column3: Disease outbreaks affect markets

column2: Parasites spread through the herd causing health problems.
column3: Parasites spread quickly in a herd. Routine veterinary checks cost a fraction of what treating an outbreak does.

column2: Flooded roads slow travel and disrupt market transport.
column3: Infrastructure damage can raise transport costs

column2: Seeds fail to grow.
column3: Quality inputs matter.

column2: Floodwaters wash away fertile topsoil from farmland.
column3: Soil protection is critical for long term farming

column2: Unsold goods spoil before they can be sold.
column3: Overproduction can lead to waste

column2: Excess production creates costly storage demands.
column3: Supply shocks can create logistical costs

column2: Transport delays cause crops to spoil before reaching market.
column3: Perishable goods have no patience for delays. A day's hold-up can turn a profit into a loss. Diversifying market access reduces this risk.

column2: Movement restrictions disrupt trade and travel.
column3: Lockdowns disrupt logistics

column2: Sick animals require veterinary care
column3: Delaying animal care to save money often leads to larger losses. Treating illness early is almost always cheaper than treating it late.

column2: Polluted water threatens livestock health.
column3: Polluted water silently damages livestock health before symptoms appear. Regular water testing is a low-cost way to avoid a high-cost crisis.

column2: Your employer pays a festive season bonus.
column3: A bonus is an opportunity, not a guarantee. Directing it toward savings, debt repayment, or insurance premiums builds lasting stability rather than temporary relief.

column2: Favorable rains result in an unusually high crop yield.
column3: Weather conditions can sometimes bring opportunity.

column2: Your burial society pays out after a difficult family period.
column3: Community financial structures provide real protection.

column2: A community project boosts local economic activity.
column3: Community development helps growth.

column2: A relative working abroad sends money home to support the household.
column3: Diversifying income sources increases resilience.

column2: A small government grant supports your operations.
column3: Opportunities reward preparation.

column2: Market prices suddenly rise, increasing profits.
column3: Windfalls from good markets are temporary. The households that benefit most are those who save a portion rather than spending the full gain.

column2: Aid organizations distribute emergency assistance during difficult times.
column3: Community and aid support can help households recover.

column2: Your employer recognises your contributions with a pay increase.
column3: Consistent performance builds financial stability.

column2: A small piece of work on the side brings in extra income this month.
column3: Multiple income streams reduce dependence on one source.

column2: An unexpected payment improves your finances.
column3: Saving windfalls builds resilience.

column2: Farming equipment was stolen when you weren't present.
column3: Asset insurance protects property.

column2: A scam results in unexpected financial loss.
column3: Financial literacy prevents fraud.

column2: Thieves steal livestock overnight on your farm.
column3: Security reduces risk.

column2: Cash was stolen during a market transaction.
column3: Diversifying cash storage matters.

column2: Your basic mobile phone was stolen. It was your only means of mobile money and communication.
column3: "A stolen phone disrupts more than communication \u2014 it cuts off financial access."

column2: Stored goods are stolen from your storage facilities.
column3: Security investment reduces risk.

column2: You were robbed while walking to the market. Your cash was taken.
column3: Carrying cash openly makes you a target. Store money safely.

column2: Goods are stolen while being transported to market.
column3: Transport security matters.
